purpose: Provides a game-theoretic framework for assessing OPEC+ stability, identifying conditions under which the cooperative equilibrium breaks down and oil prices deviate from the managed band
components: Fiscal breakeven thresholds: Saudi $75-90/bbl, UAE $60-75, Iraq/Nigeria $95-110
Spare capacity buffer: 4-5Mb/d creating managed $70-90 Brent band
Conditional probability: <3% spare capacity threshold with 67% hit rate for price spikes
2018 US shale structural break altered the game theory
current_assessment: OPEC+ is in a structurally fragile cooperative equilibrium. Oil functions as a managed market with a ceiling, making it unsuitable for supercycle classification. Wrong-way risk properties mean breakdown is most likely when fiscal pressures are highest.
limitations: Fiscal breakeven thresholds shift over time with government spending
US shale response function is evolving (capital discipline vs. production growth)
Geopolitical disruption scenarios could overwhelm spare capacity
Does not model sanctions effects on individual members
